info.name: MyCourse Backend API
info.description:
REST API for **be-mycourse** (Gin). Responses use envelope `{ "code", "message", "data" }` with `code: 0` for success.
Health check uses `{ "code", "message", "status" }`.
CSRF protection logic uses double-submit cookie: cookie `csrf_token` + header `X-CSRF-Token` for unsafe methods (`POST`, `PUT`, `PATCH`, `DELETE`).
Temporary rollout note: router-level CSRF enforcement is currently disabled.

**Audit timestamps:** `created_at`, `updated_at`, and soft-delete `deleted_at` (where present) are **Unix epoch seconds** (`integer` / `int64` in JSON), not ISO-8601 strings. DB columns are PostgreSQL `BIGINT` (migration `000011`).

**Apidog / Postman — auto-save tokens:** After **Login**, **Confirm email**, or **Refresh**, post-processors in `x-postman-event` set `ACCESS_TOKEN`, `REFRESH_TOKEN`, `SESSION_ID` (from JSON `data` or cookies). Select an **Environment** with those variables before other requests. **Bearer** auth uses `{{ACCESS_TOKEN}}`. **System token:** obtain via `CLI_SYSTEM_LOGIN=1 go run .` (stdout only) and set `SYSTEM_TOKEN` manually in your environment.


Regenerate this file: `ruby scripts/generate-apidog-postman.rb` (token scripts + examples from `docs/api_swagger.yaml` only).
